blocks.html_en:
Those of us who liked the open web and P2P and hacking on projects all the time are seeing these models as a way to rebuild the internet in a shape that pleases us. To build anything and everything we want. Sans opportunity cost.

And that's the real value - you don't have to burn the candle of your life away to get what you want. You'll have so much more time now that you aren't AST wrangling.

Opportunity cost is vanishing (or at least the cost is multiple orders of magnitude smaller). And that's the most exciting thing that's happened in tech since the internet.

I'll probably be able to make my own phone operating system.

I'll probably be able to decompile N64 games, port them to Rust, combine them, extend them. In a weekend.

I'll probably be able to make my own music management platform while simultaneously working on a few robotics projects.

A find my phone button.

Automate everything.

And that's maybe a month of projects.

sentences.en: Those of us who liked the open web and P2P and hacking on projects all the time are seeing these models as a way to rebuild the internet in a shape that pleases us.
sentences.zh: 我们这些喜欢开放网络、P2P 以及一直对项目进行黑客攻击的人将这些模型视为以我们满意的形式重建互联网的一种方式。

sentences.en: To build anything and everything we want.
sentences.zh: 建造我们想要的任何东西。

sentences.en: Sans opportunity cost.And that's the real value - you don't have to burn the candle of your life away to get what you want.
sentences.zh: 没有机会成本。这就是真正的价值——你不必为了得到你想要的东西而烧掉你生命中的蜡烛。

sentences.en: You'll have so much more time now that you aren't AST wrangling.Opportunity cost is vanishing (or at least the cost is multiple orders of magnitude smaller).
sentences.zh: 现在您将拥有更多时间，不再需要 AST 争论。机会成本正在消失（或者至少成本要小多个数量级）。

sentences.en: And that's the most exciting thing that's happened in tech since the internet.I'll probably be able to make my own phone operating system.I'll probably be able to decompile N64 games, port them to Rust, combine them, extend them.
sentences.zh: 这是自互联网以来科技领域发生的最令人兴奋的事情。我可能能够制作自己的手机操作系统。我可能能够反编译 N64 游戏，将它们移植到 Rust，组合它们，扩展它们。

sentences.en: In a weekend.I'll probably be able to make my own music management platform while simultaneously working on a few robotics projects.A find my phone button.Automate everything.And that's maybe a month of projects.
sentences.zh: 在一个周末。我可能能够制作自己的音乐管理平台，同时处理一些机器人项目。找到我的电话按钮。自动化一切。这可能是一个月的项目。

blocks.html_en: The whole thing feels like that the US collectively isn't feeling confident about itself in the coming creativity-limited world that AI/AGIs supposedly bring, and that it's not happy about that pessimistic rational projections. I don't know exactly why wouldn't it be or why nothing seem to be done about it, though. Both look to be fundamentally solvable problems to me.

sentences.en: The whole thing feels like that the US collectively isn't feeling confident about itself in the coming creativity-limited world that AI/AGIs supposedly bring, and that it's not happy about that pessimistic rational projections.
sentences.zh: 整个事情给人的感觉是，在人工智能/通用人工智能所带来的即将到来的创造力有限的世界中，美国集体对自己没有信心，而且对这种悲观的理性预测并不满意。

sentences.en: I don't know exactly why wouldn't it be or why nothing seem to be done about it, though.
sentences.zh: 但我不知道为什么不会，或者为什么似乎没有采取任何措施。

sentences.en: Both look to be fundamentally solvable problems to me.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这两个问题都是从根本上可以解决的。

> You'll have so much more time now that you aren't AST wrangling.

So much time because you were fired and have nothing to do. Nothing is as good as oodles of quality time worrying about funding your family's needs. It's amazing.

sentences.en: Nothing is as good as oodles of quality time worrying about funding your family's needs.
sentences.zh: 没有什么比花大量的美好时间来担心家人的需要更好的了。

sentences.en: It's amazing.
sentences.zh: 太棒了。

blocks.html_en:
Robots will take jobs from massive amount of low income people.

AI will take jobs from a massive amount of avg and high income people.

The disruption ahead is getting pillowed right now due to physical limitations of progress like amount of energy, building DC, doing research, training models (its still takes 1-3 month).

Also a massive buffer will be the boomer going into retirement.

Depending on the speed of all of this, we might get a lot poorer before we get abundance everyerwer

sentences.en: Robots will take jobs from massive amount of low income people.AI will take jobs from a massive amount of avg and high income people.The disruption ahead is getting pillowed right now due to physical limitations of progress like amount of energy, building DC, doing research, training models (its still takes 1-3 month).Also a massive buffer will be the boomer going into retirement.Depending on the speed of all of this, we might get a lot poorer before we get abundance everyerwer
sentences.zh: 机器人将抢走大量低收入人群的工作。人工智能将从大量平均收入和高收入人群中抢走工作。由于能源、建设数据中心、研究、训练模型等进步的物理限制，未来的颠覆现在正在被掩盖。此外，婴儿潮一代将进入退休状态，这将是一个巨大的缓冲。根据这一切的速度，在我们变得富裕之前，我们可能会变得更加贫穷。

blocks.html_en:
You have no evidence of this.

Also: you can literally make incredible software that would take half a year right now for $200/month. Or free if you run local open weights.

You can do branding, marketing, design. You can create comics and movies and video games.

You can make an AI tool right now and get customers today. You have no idea how wildly popular this segment is. How much people willingly engage with this stuff and want it.

Yet you choose to disengage and complain about a future that hasn't happened.

It's more likely that firms will need to hire more to stay competitive. Or that workers will fire their old jobs and break out on their own.

Something I used to think was impossible: competing with Google, Apple, Stripe. Not anymore. I think you can compete with anyone.

sentences.en: Not anymore.
sentences.zh: 不再。

sentences.en: I think you can compete with anyone.

blocks.html_en:
I saw robots doing this in tech demos 15 years ago. And even those robots weren't doing anything new. Like all AI models, this is just AI repackaging and rebranding of things that robots have already been programmed to do using more classical control methods. The idea to do everything via AI was of course thought of for a long time, but the conclusion has been we have faster and more reliable methods to do the same thing with less compute, and that's still true.

During my undergrad I worked on a project where we set off a bunch of walking robots, recorded their walks, and then used the data as the basis for a learned walking controller. I see this Xiaomi model as pretty much like that.

It's a nice technology and maybe could lead to better folding one day but unfortunately it's not "finally we have robots that can do our laundry" (yet).

sentences.en: I saw robots doing this in tech demos 15 years ago.
sentences.zh: 15 年前，我在技术演示中看到机器人这样做了。

sentences.en: And even those robots weren't doing anything new.
sentences.zh: 甚至那些机器人也没有做任何新的事情。

sentences.en: Like all AI models, this is just AI repackaging and rebranding of things that robots have already been programmed to do using more classical control methods.
sentences.zh: 与所有人工智能模型一样，这只是机器人已经被编程为使用更经典的控制方法来完成的事情的人工智能重新包装和品牌重塑。

sentences.en: The idea to do everything via AI was of course thought of for a long time, but the conclusion has been we have faster and more reliable methods to do the same thing with less compute, and that's still true.During my undergrad I worked on a project where we set off a bunch of walking robots, recorded their walks, and then used the data as the basis for a learned walking controller.
sentences.zh: 通过人工智能完成所有事情的想法当然已经被考虑了很长时间，但结论是我们有更快、更可靠的方法来用更少的计算来做同样的事情，这仍然是事实。在我本科期间，我参与了一个项目，我们启动了一群行走机器人，记录它们的行走，然后使用这些数据作为学习行走控制器的基础。

sentences.en: I see this Xiaomi model as pretty much like that.It's a nice technology and maybe could lead to better folding one day but unfortunately it's not "finally we have robots that can do our laundry" (yet).
sentences.zh: 我认为这款小米型号与此非常相似。这是一项很好的技术，也许有一天可以带来更好的折叠效果，但不幸的是，它还不是“我们终于有了可以洗衣服的机器人”。

blocks.html_en:
> You're telling me we've finally got robots that can do our laundry?

I’m still waiting for it to peel, slice n dice onions so that I do not have to cry while doing so, and not pay $8k for that robot.

sentences.en: Covering the last 20% can take your entire lifespan
sentences.zh: 覆盖最后 20% 可能需要您的一生

blocks.html_en: Is a Chinese robot vacuuming and mopping my floor every day? Yes it is. Could I do it better manually? Absolutely. I still prefer the robot to do an ok job than to do it myself.

sentences.en: Is a Chinese robot vacuuming and mopping my floor every day?
sentences.zh: 中国的机器人每天都会给我吸尘、拖地吗？

sentences.en: Yes it is.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: Could I do it better manually?
sentences.zh: 我可以手动做得更好吗？

sentences.en: Absolutely.
sentences.zh: 绝对地。

sentences.en: I still prefer the robot to do an ok job than to do it myself.
sentences.zh: 我还是更喜欢机器人把工作做好，而不是自己做。

sentences.en: Folding 7 times doesn't eliminate wrinkles.
sentences.zh: 折叠7次并不能消除皱纹。

blocks.html_en:
Most people in software misunderstand how physical goods work. When making machines you don't care about getting everything perfect all the time, you care about staying within tolerable limits all the time.

How bad do the folds in your laundry need to be for them to be no different then just being dumped in a pile? As long as we are above that point the robot is useful.

sentences.en: Most people in software misunderstand how physical goods work.
sentences.zh: 大多数软件行业的人都误解了实体产品的工作原理。

sentences.en: When making machines you don't care about getting everything perfect all the time, you care about staying within tolerable limits all the time.How bad do the folds in your laundry need to be for them to be no different then just being dumped in a pile?
sentences.zh: 在制造机器时，您并不关心让一切始终完美，您关心的是始终保持在可容忍的范围内。衣物中的褶皱需要有多严重才能使它们与被扔成一堆没有什么不同？

sentences.en: As long as we are above that point the robot is useful.
sentences.zh: 只要我们达到这一点，机器人就有用。

I don’t need it to do it faster than me, I just need it to do it instead of me.

Plus it’s obvious it will get better.

blocks.html_en:
> Super slow and bad at it

Eh, this still has value for me if it can do it reliably. Leaving a robot to badly fold my t-shirts overnight and then badly put them in my closet the next day is fine. I don't need my casual wear beautifully folded. But I do need it organised, and that's apparently a chore I'll just never learn to like doing.

sentences.en: > Super slow and bad at itEh, this still has value for me if it can do it reliably.
sentences.zh: > 超级慢而且不擅长呃，如果它能可靠地做到这一点，这对我来说仍然有价值。

sentences.en: Leaving a robot to badly fold my t-shirts overnight and then badly put them in my closet the next day is fine.
sentences.zh: 让机器人在一夜之间把我的 T 恤折得很糟糕，然后第二天又把它们很糟糕地放进我的衣柜里，这也没什么问题。

sentences.en: I don't need my casual wear beautifully folded.
sentences.zh: 我不需要我的休闲服折叠得很漂亮。

sentences.en: But I do need it organised, and that's apparently a chore I'll just never learn to like doing.
sentences.zh: 但我确实需要把它组织起来，而这显然是一件我永远不会喜欢做的苦差事。

blocks.html_en:
The real question is whether a hardware platform today would be good enough.

The model is software on a server or in the cloud, so the real question is are there any show stoppers from the hardware for going faster?

blocks.html_en: My solution: stuff the clothes from the washer into the dryer, then stuff them into my wardrobe. I buy clothes that don't get wrinkled much even if simply shoved in the wardrobe and if they do, I tolerate it and wear them anyway.

sentences.en: My solution: stuff the clothes from the washer into the dryer, then stuff them into my wardrobe.
sentences.zh: 我的解决办法：把洗衣机里的衣服塞进烘干机，然后塞进我的衣柜。

sentences.en: I buy clothes that don't get wrinkled much even if simply shoved in the wardrobe and if they do, I tolerate it and wear them anyway.
sentences.zh: 我买的衣服即使只是塞进衣柜也不会起皱，即使起皱，我也能忍受并穿着它们。

blocks.html_en: Ha! You remind me of something I used to tell my colleagues back when I held a regular job. They would compliment me on my highly patterned shirts (eg. A very small floral pattern repeated). I would explain that a shirt like that just requires low spin in the washer, and then drying on a hanger. Whatever wrinkles are left are almost invisible due to the pattern. With collars and cuffs I’d just literally manually stretch/tension them while they were still wet. Bada bing bada boom.

sentences.en: Ha!

blocks.html_en: This is a mostly American perspective that I find endlessly amusing. I only have a handful of clothing items that say that tumble drying is fine on the tag, and most of my clothing is very casual wear (t-shirts, jeans, sports clothing). Dryers eat up clothing like it's nobody's business. Plus, electricity costs money while the sun is free. And while yes, the time to hang laundry has value, it takes 10 minutes to hang a load. If it rains, I hang the clothes indoors with a dehumidifier which is cheaper to run than a dryer and dries clothes in a matter of a couple of hours.

sentences.en: This is a mostly American perspective that I find endlessly amusing.
sentences.zh: 这是一种大多数美国人的观点，我觉得非常有趣。

sentences.en: Dryers eat up clothing like it's nobody's business.
sentences.zh: 烘干机会把衣服吃掉，就像这与任何人无关一样。

sentences.en: Plus, electricity costs money while the sun is free.
sentences.zh: 另外，电费要花钱，而阳光是免费的。

sentences.zh: 是的，晾衣服的时间很有价值，但晾衣服需要 10 分钟。

sentences.en: If it rains, I hang the clothes indoors with a dehumidifier which is cheaper to run than a dryer and dries clothes in a matter of a couple of hours.
sentences.zh: 如果下雨，我会用除湿机将衣服挂在室内，这种除湿机的运行成本比烘干机便宜，并且在几个小时内就能烘干衣服。

blocks.html_en:
> I'm not sure why some comments here are so pessimistic.

Actually, it seems like 99% of the comments are glazing this with zero skepticism actually. If anything the comments are too enthusiastic and lack the measured discernment that was more common on HN 5+ years ago.

blocks.html_en:
> This is what robotics should be used for.

I disagree. We should use robotics to extend our reach into hazardous environments, enhance our physical capabilities, or to provide a steady handling of things where it's absolutely needed - doing laundry in my household is none of these.

Folding laundry takes me 10 mins every 10 days. I organize my closet once in a while according to my sense of fashion, and now I'll just need to keep that structure. I don't always like to do these chores, but I have no issue in acknowledging them as part of my responsibilities. Why would I even automate[0] that?

Robotics is costly, and I see no way how that would change anytime soon. That isn't a flaw of some kind either, it's rather just one of the limitations that needs to be accounted for, neglecting that would be detrimental to the future I envision.

Don't get me wrong, I love robotics with a passion, as engineer I like to tinker, and I'm a big admirer of FESTOs bionic research[1]. It's just that the field suffers from an "overpromised and underdelivered" aura as far as I can remember, and I remember the presentation of ASIMO[2] 26 years ago.

Some people would like to see the field hyped up, focused on the consumer like every other field in tech, probably for financial gain, but I personally see the value in its downplay. To be honest, I love these reality checks, as I see them as a valuable part of the learning process all together - a journey for what it means to automate, and what it means to define as human natural chore.

[0]: https://xkcd.com/1205/

[1]: https://www.festo.com/us/en/e/about-festo/research-and-devel...

[2]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ASIMO

sentences.en: > This is what robotics should be used for.I disagree.
sentences.zh: > 这才是机器人应该被使用的地方。我不同意。

sentences.en: We should use robotics to extend our reach into hazardous environments, enhance our physical capabilities, or to provide a steady handling of things where it's absolutely needed - doing laundry in my household is none of these.Folding laundry takes me 10 mins every 10 days.
sentences.zh: 我们应该使用机器人技术将我们的触角延伸到危险环境中，增强我们的身体能力，或者在绝对需要的地方稳定地处理事情——在我家里洗衣服都不是这些。每 10 天折叠衣服需要我花 10 分钟。

sentences.en: I organize my closet once in a while according to my sense of fashion, and now I'll just need to keep that structure.
sentences.zh: 我偶尔会根据自己的时尚感整理一下衣柜，现在我只需要保持这种结构即可。

sentences.en: I don't always like to do these chores, but I have no issue in acknowledging them as part of my responsibilities.
sentences.zh: 我并不总是喜欢做这些家务，但我毫不犹豫地承认它们是我职责的一部分。

sentences.en: Why would I even automate[0] that?Robotics is costly, and I see no way how that would change anytime soon.
sentences.zh: 为什么我要自动化[0]？机器人成本高昂，而且我认为这种情况短期内不会改变。

sentences.en: That isn't a flaw of some kind either, it's rather just one of the limitations that needs to be accounted for, neglecting that would be detrimental to the future I envision.Don't get me wrong, I love robotics with a passion, as engineer I like to tinker, and I'm a big admirer of FESTOs bionic research[1].
sentences.zh: 这也不是某种缺陷，而只是需要考虑的限制之一，忽视这一点将对我设想的未来不利。不要误会我的意思，我充满热情地热爱机器人技术，作为工程师，我喜欢修补，而且我非常崇拜 FESTO 的仿生研究 [1]。

sentences.en: It's just that the field suffers from an "overpromised and underdelivered" aura as far as I can remember, and I remember the presentation of ASIMO[2] 26 years ago.Some people would like to see the field hyped up, focused on the consumer like every other field in tech, probably for financial gain, but I personally see the value in its downplay.
sentences.zh: 只是在我的记忆中，这个领域一直笼罩着一种“承诺过多、交付不足”的光环，我还记得 26 年前 ASIMO[2] 的演讲。有些人希望看到这个领域被大肆炒作，像其他科技领域一样关注消费者，可能是为了经济利益，但我个人认为淡化它的价值。

sentences.en: To be honest, I love these reality checks, as I see them as a valuable part of the learning process all together - a journey for what it means to automate, and what it means to define as human natural chore.[0]: https://xkcd.com/1205/[1]: https://www.festo.com/us/en/e/about-festo/research-and-devel...[2]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ASIMO

blocks.html_en:
> This is what robotics should be used for.

I disagree. We should use robotics to extend our reach into hazardous environments, enhance our physical capabilities, or to provide a steady handling of things where it's absolutely needed - doing laundry in my household is none of these.

Folding laundry takes me 10 mins every 10 days. I organize my closet once in a while according to my sense of fashion, and now I'll just need to keep that structure. I don't always like to do these chores, but I have no issue in acknowledging them as part of my responsibilities. Why would I even automate[0] that?

Robotics is costly, and I see no way how that would change anytime soon. That isn't a flaw of some kind either, it's rather just one of the limitations that needs to be accounted for, neglecting that would be detrimental to the future I envision.

Don't get me wrong, I love robotics with a passion, as engineer I like to tinker, and I'm a big admirer of FESTOs bionic research[1]. It's just that the field suffers from an "overpromised and underdelivered" aura as far as I can remember, and I remember the presentation of ASIMO[2] 26 years ago.

Some people would like to see the field hyped up, focused on the consumer like every other field in tech, probably for financial gain, but I personally see the value in its downplay. To be honest, I love these reality checks, as I see them as a valuable part of the learning process all together - a journey for what it means to automate, and what it means to define as human natural chore.

[0]: https://xkcd.com/1205/

[1]: https://www.festo.com/us/en/e/about-festo/research-and-devel...

[2]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ASIMO

blocks.html_en:
> I'm not sure why some comments here are so pessimistic.

Time and time and time and time and time again, technologists are always naïve to never realize that the road to hell is paved with good intentions.

I am expecting the opposite effect to happen with the actual use of robotics. Mass displacement of every sector of labor.

This is not ruled out.

sentences.zh: 各个劳动部门都会大规模流离失所。不排除这种情况。

blocks.html_en:
A subset of the people on HN hate AI and yell "slop" at everything they see. It's even worse if you go to other social websites.

Just ignore these people. If they don't use AI, they likely won't remain practicing in our field for much longer. They will naturally self-select themselves out of relevancy.

I was Claude coding and got hit by the 3 AM reset bug. I went on reddit and saw hundreds of other engineers hit by the same. We are different people than the AI haters, and there has never been something so powerful it's kept me up past 3 AM every single night working beyond every fiber of my body telling me to sleep. I feel empowered as if I command an army. And there are millions of us. The sheer might of all of this will win out over negativity.

I've built multiple successful businesses in the last year. If you're doing this with any sort of interest in self-employment you don't need your employer anymore.

I have never been happier. This is the best future, and I feel lucky to be living it. It's what I dreamed about when I was a kid, and it feels magical. As far as I can tell, the future has no limits.

sentences.en: A subset of the people on HN hate AI and yell "slop" at everything they see.
sentences.zh: HN 上的一部分人讨厌人工智能，并对他们看到的一切大喊“垃圾”。

sentences.en: It's even worse if you go to other social websites.Just ignore these people.
sentences.zh: 如果你去其他社交网站那就更糟糕了。忽略这些人就可以了。

sentences.en: If they don't use AI, they likely won't remain practicing in our field for much longer.
sentences.zh: 如果他们不使用人工智能，他们可能不会在我们的领域继续实践太久。

sentences.en: They will naturally self-select themselves out of relevancy.I was Claude coding and got hit by the 3 AM reset bug.
sentences.zh: 他们会自然而然地自我选择脱离相关性。我是克劳德编码，并受到凌晨 3 点重置错误的影响。

sentences.en: I went on reddit and saw hundreds of other engineers hit by the same.
sentences.zh: 我在 Reddit 上看到其他数百名工程师也遇到了同样的问题。

sentences.en: We are different people than the AI haters, and there has never been something so powerful it's kept me up past 3 AM every single night working beyond every fiber of my body telling me to sleep.
sentences.zh: 我们和那些讨厌人工智能的人是不同的人，从来没有什么东西如此强大，它让我每晚都熬夜到凌晨 3 点，超出了我身体的每一根纤维告诉我睡觉的范围。

sentences.en: I feel empowered as if I command an army.
sentences.zh: 我感觉自己被赋予了权力，就好像我指挥着一支军队一样。

sentences.en: And there are millions of us.
sentences.zh: 我们有数百万人。

sentences.en: The sheer might of all of this will win out over negativity.I've built multiple successful businesses in the last year.
sentences.zh: 所有这一切的绝对力量将战胜消极情绪。去年我已经建立了多个成功的企业。

sentences.en: If you're doing this with any sort of interest in self-employment you don't need your employer anymore.I have never been happier.
sentences.zh: 如果你这样做是出于对自营职业的兴趣，你就不再需要你的雇主了。我从来没有这么高兴过。

sentences.en: This is the best future, and I feel lucky to be living it.
sentences.zh: 这是最好的未来，我为能生活在其中而感到幸运。

sentences.en: It's what I dreamed about when I was a kid, and it feels magical.
sentences.zh: 这是我小时候的梦想，感觉很神奇。

sentences.en: As far as I can tell, the future has no limits.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，未来没有极限。

blocks.html_en:
A subset of the people on HN hate AI and yell "slop" at everything they see. It's even worse if you go to other social websites.

Just ignore these people. If they don't use AI, they likely won't remain practicing in our field for much longer. They will naturally self-select themselves out of relevancy.

I was Claude coding and got hit by the 3 AM reset bug. I went on reddit and saw hundreds of other engineers hit by the same. We are different people than the AI haters, and there has never been something so powerful it's kept me up past 3 AM every single night working beyond every fiber of my body telling me to sleep. I feel empowered as if I command an army. And there are millions of us. The sheer might of all of this will win out over negativity.

I've built multiple successful businesses in the last year. If you're doing this with any sort of interest in self-employment you don't need your employer anymore.

I have never been happier. This is the best future, and I feel lucky to be living it. It's what I dreamed about when I was a kid, and it feels magical. As far as I can tell, the future has no limits.

blocks.html_en: It's always funny seeing people that write like this as if they are somehow exempt from the future we're seemingly rushing into. Bro, you prooompting yourself into unconsciousness is not going to make you not obsolete. Unless you're being sarcastic in which case poe's law.

blocks.html_en:
Working past your personal bedtime, part of an imaginary movement of millions might, ignoring the negative aspects of AI, empowered like commanding an army. This frankly sound psychotic, and it's exactly why uncontrolled AI usage is seen so critical by many.

And all other devs will leave the field just because they prefer to work differently? That is not only not realistic, it is violence fuel.

sentences.en: Working past your personal bedtime, part of an imaginary movement of millions might, ignoring the negative aspects of AI, empowered like commanding an army.
sentences.zh: 在你个人的就寝时间之后工作，这是数百万人可能想象的运动的一部分，忽略人工智能的负面影响，像指挥一支军队一样获得权力。

sentences.en: This frankly sound psychotic, and it's exactly why uncontrolled AI usage is seen so critical by many.And all other devs will leave the field just because they prefer to work differently?
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，这听起来很精神病，这正是许多人认为不受控制的人工智能使用如此重要的原因。所有其他开发人员都会离开该领域，只是因为他们更喜欢以不同的方式工作？

sentences.en: That is not only not realistic, it is violence fuel.
sentences.zh: 这不仅不现实，而且还会助长暴力。

blocks.html_en:
You will have to work long nights when you try to build a business of your own while continuing to work a regular job.

Talking about psychosis and "violence fuel" while handwaving at negative aspects of AI does not seem substantial.

I would be more curious to hear details about how OP built the claimed businesses.

I will not see this info here if people who build things and are enthusiastic about AI are driven away by downvotes and negative comments.

sentences.en: You will have to work long nights when you try to build a business of your own while continuing to work a regular job.Talking about psychosis and "violence fuel" while handwaving at negative aspects of AI does not seem substantial.I would be more curious to hear details about how OP built the claimed businesses.I will not see this info here if people who build things and are enthusiastic about AI are driven away by downvotes and negative comments.
sentences.zh: 当你试图建立自己的企业，同时继续做一份常规工作时，你将不得不通宵达旦地工作。在谈论精神病和“暴力燃料”的同时对人工智能的负面方面挥手示意似乎并不重要。我更想知道OP如何建立所声称的企业的细节。如果那些创造东西并对人工智能充满热情的人被反对票和负面评论赶走，我不会在这里看到这些信息。

blocks.html_en:
The frustrating part is that these posts will never post any details about the business, despite the fact that it would immediately invalidate most criticism.

It seems to be a similar phenomenon to productivity "gurus" who write and evangelize polyphasic sleep and "grindset" and so on - when you ask what they're using these superpowers to actually accomplish, they're very quiet. Or it's "coming soon." Or "I'm not going to waste time explaining to someone who obviously doesn't ~believe~." It quickly becomes religious.

sentences.en: The frustrating part is that these posts will never post any details about the business, despite the fact that it would immediately invalidate most criticism.It seems to be a similar phenomenon to productivity "gurus" who write and evangelize polyphasic sleep and "grindset" and so on - when you ask what they're using these superpowers to actually accomplish, they're very quiet.
sentences.zh: 令人沮丧的是，这些帖子永远不会发布有关业务的任何细节，尽管事实上这会立即使大多数批评无效。这似乎与生产力“大师”的现象类似，他们撰写和宣传多相睡眠和“磨砺”等 - 当你问他们正在使用这些超能力来实际完成什么时，他们非常安静。

sentences.en: Or it's "coming soon."
sentences.zh: 或者说“即将到来”。

sentences.en: Or "I'm not going to waste time explaining to someone who obviously doesn't ~believe~."
sentences.zh: 或者“我不会浪费时间向显然不相信的人解释。”

sentences.en: It quickly becomes religious.
sentences.zh: 它很快就变得宗教化了。

sentences.zh: 这似乎与生产力“大师”的现象类似，他们撰写并宣传多相睡眠和“磨砺”等——当你问他们正在使用这些超能力来实际完成什么时，他们非常安静。

sentences.en: Or it's "coming soon."
sentences.zh: 或者说“即将到来”。

sentences.en: Or "I'm not going to waste time explaining to someone who obviously doesn't ~believe~."
sentences.zh: 或者“我不会浪费时间向显然不相信的人解释。”

sentences.en: It quickly becomes religious.
sentences.zh: 它很快就变得宗教化了。

blocks.html_en:
You're not going to be able to keep up with the market if you're not using AI.

100kloc in a day is table stakes now. And that's just where we are today - imagine a year from now.

You can't continue to be a horse cart driver and get paid for it. Or an abacus user. We're moving past it now.

sentences.en: We're moving past it now.

blocks.html_en:
Even if you're Fabrice Bellard, you can't compete with how fast these models are. They'll run circles around any human.

I don't know what else to tell you other than you should try the models again and update your assumptions.

A year ago I thought LLMs were trash. This year has been a completely eye opening experience.

Do not be so stubborn that your opinions are unshakable. Something exceptional is happening and it deserves just a little bit more of your attention.

sentences.en: Even if you're Fabrice Bellard, you can't compete with how fast these models are.
sentences.zh: 即使您是法布里斯·贝拉德 (Fabrice Bellard)，您也无法与这些模型的速度相竞争。

sentences.en: They'll run circles around any human.I don't know what else to tell you other than you should try the models again and update your assumptions.A year ago I thought LLMs were trash.
sentences.zh: 他们会绕着任何人转圈。除了你应该再次尝试这些模型并更新你的假设之外，我不知道还能告诉你什么。一年前，我认为法学硕士是垃圾。

sentences.en: This year has been a completely eye opening experience.Do not be so stubborn that your opinions are unshakable.
sentences.zh: 今年真是令人大开眼界的经历。不要固执到你的观点不可动摇。

sentences.en: Something exceptional is happening and it deserves just a little bit more of your attention.
sentences.zh: 一些特殊的事情正在发生，值得你多加一点关注。

blocks.html_en:
> there has never been something so powerful it's kept me up past 3 AM every single night working beyond every fiber of my body telling me to sleep. I feel empowered as if I command an army. And there are millions of us

Yelling "slop" at a clothes-folding robot is stupid, but reading this kind of shit makes me want to embody Ted Kaczynszky and go live in the woods.

sentences.en: > there has never been something so powerful it's kept me up past 3 AM every single night working beyond every fiber of my body telling me to sleep.
sentences.zh: > 从未有过如此强大的力量，让我每晚都熬夜到凌晨 3 点，超越了我身体的每一根纤维告诉我该睡觉的程度。

sentences.en: I feel empowered as if I command an army.
sentences.zh: 我感觉自己被赋予了权力，就好像我指挥着一支军队一样。

sentences.en: And there are millions of usYelling "slop" at a clothes-folding robot is stupid, but reading this kind of shit makes me want to embody Ted Kaczynszky and go live in the woods.
sentences.zh: 有数以百万计的人对着折叠衣服的机器人大喊“噗”是愚蠢的，但读到这种狗屎让我想化身特德·卡钦斯基并去树林里生活。

blocks.html_en: We nearly destroyed our planet to get there. Technocrats here applaude the new technological achievements that make life a little easier (less boring?) for the top one percent while willfully ignoring the associated costs for everyone else. Manufacturing these at scale will only make things worse. We are approaching peak decadence ever faster.

sentences.en: We nearly destroyed our planet to get there.
sentences.zh: 为了到达那里，我们几乎摧毁了我们的星球。

sentences.en: Technocrats here applaude the new technological achievements that make life a little easier (less boring?)
sentences.zh: 这里的技术官僚对新技术成就表示赞赏，这些成就让生活变得更轻松（不那么无聊？）

sentences.en: for the top one percent while willfully ignoring the associated costs for everyone else.
sentences.zh: 为最顶层的百分之一的人提供服务，而故意忽视其他人的相关成本。

sentences.en: Manufacturing these at scale will only make things worse.
sentences.zh: 大规模生产这些只会让事情变得更糟。

sentences.en: We are approaching peak decadence ever faster.
sentences.zh: 我们正以更快的速度接近颓废的顶峰。

blocks.html_en:
I am of course impressed by the model and the robot. Great stuff,

But the downside is that won't we all just turn into the obese, stupid humans at the start of the WALL-E film?

Mindfulness teaches us that we can enjoy the washing up, the laundry, planting seeds, tending the garden. It connects us to something. Without this will we end up with a rise in mental health issues, because for one thing people will just end up on social media in the time they save.

Just a thought.

sentences.en: I am of course impressed by the model and the robot.
sentences.zh: 当然，模型和机器人给我留下了深刻的印象。

sentences.en: Great stuff,But the downside is that won't we all just turn into the obese, stupid humans at the start of the WALL-E film?Mindfulness teaches us that we can enjoy the washing up, the laundry, planting seeds, tending the garden.
sentences.zh: 很棒的东西，但缺点是我们不会都变成电影《机器人总动员》电影开头那样肥胖、愚蠢的人类吗？正念告诉我们，我们可以享受洗碗、洗衣服、播种、照料花园的乐趣。

sentences.en: It connects us to something.
sentences.zh: 它将我们与某些事物联系起来。

sentences.en: Without this will we end up with a rise in mental health issues, because for one thing people will just end up on social media in the time they save.Just a thought.
sentences.zh: 如果没有这一点，我们最终会导致心理健康问题的增加，因为一方面，人们在节省下来的时间里最终只会出现在社交媒体上。只是一个想法。

Without this will we end up with a rise in mental health issues, because for one thing people will just end up on social media in the time they save.

sentences.en: Mindfulness teaches us that we can enjoy the washing up, the laundry, planting seeds, tending the garden.
sentences.zh: 正念告诉我们，我们可以享受洗碗、洗衣、播种、照料花园的乐趣。

sentences.en: It connects us to something.
sentences.zh: 它将我们与某些事物联系起来。

sentences.en: Without this will we end up with a rise in mental health issues, because for one thing people will just end up on social media in the time they save.
sentences.zh: 如果没有这一点，我们最终会导致心理健康问题的增加，因为一方面，人们最终会在节省下来的时间里使用社交媒体。

blocks.html_en: Just a thought.

sentences.en: Just a thought.
sentences.zh: 只是一个想法。

Maybe if you can shave an hour every day from chores, you will actually have the energy to enjoy physical activities

blocks.html_en:
I often wonder if they just made us do more washing, use more cups, travel further in the same time and get more expectations from our boss though.

I've got 2 kids. I'm always doing laundry and loading the dishwasher. How much of that is just higher expectations of cleanliness? Back when washing clothes involved going to the river all day, kids would get sewn into their clothes for winter. These days they have 5 outfit changes a day. I'm certain that my dishwasher means everyone just grabs a clean mug/glass/bowl rather than just rinsing a dirty one. I use a computer all day to get stuff done. But that just means my boss expects more stuff to be done. The baseline shifted.

A few hundred years ago people mostly lived near where they were born and didn't travel much. Now we get in our cars to drive a few hours to visit our family and friends. But they only moved far away because the car enabled it.

Obviously there are benefits - especially for those who find dirt upsetting - but have we actually changed the balance of leisure time very much?

sentences.en: I often wonder if they just made us do more washing, use more cups, travel further in the same time and get more expectations from our boss though.I've got 2 kids.
sentences.zh: 我常常想，他们是否只是让我们多洗衣服，多用杯子，在同一时间走更远的路，并从我们的老板那里得到更多的期望。我有 2 个孩子。

sentences.en: I'm always doing laundry and loading the dishwasher.
sentences.zh: 我总是洗衣服并放入洗碗机。

sentences.en: How much of that is just higher expectations of cleanliness?
sentences.zh: 其中有多少只是对清洁度的更高期望？

sentences.en: Back when washing clothes involved going to the river all day, kids would get sewn into their clothes for winter.
sentences.zh: 以前洗衣服需要整天到河边去，孩子们会被缝进冬天的衣服里。

sentences.en: These days they have 5 outfit changes a day.
sentences.zh: 现在他们每天换五套衣服。

sentences.en: I'm certain that my dishwasher means everyone just grabs a clean mug/glass/bowl rather than just rinsing a dirty one.
sentences.zh: 我确信我的洗碗机意味着每个人都只需拿一个干净的杯子/玻璃杯/碗，而不是仅仅冲洗脏的杯子/玻璃杯/碗。

sentences.en: I use a computer all day to get stuff done.
sentences.zh: 我整天使用电脑来完成工作。

sentences.en: But that just means my boss expects more stuff to be done.
sentences.zh: 但这只是意味着我的老板希望完成更多的事情。

sentences.en: The baseline shifted.A few hundred years ago people mostly lived near where they were born and didn't travel much.
sentences.zh: 基线发生了变化。几百年前，人们大多住在出生地附近，很少旅行。

sentences.en: Now we get in our cars to drive a few hours to visit our family and friends.
sentences.zh: 现在我们开车几个小时去拜访我们的家人和朋友。

sentences.en: But they only moved far away because the car enabled it.Obviously there are benefits - especially for those who find dirt upsetting - but have we actually changed the balance of leisure time very much?
sentences.zh: 但他们只是搬到很远的地方，因为汽车启用了它。显然有好处 - 特别是对于那些发现污垢令人不安的人 - 但我们实际上已经极大地改变了休闲时间的平衡吗？

blocks.html_en:
I mean I like the much lower rates of dysentery and untreatable skin and dental conditions...

People have an extremely rosey view of the past shaped by the fact that period dramas are acted by modern Hollywood actors.

blocks.html_en:
This discourse reminds me of the handful of Christians who thought we needed Corn Flakes and uncomfortable clothing to stay chaste and that electricity made us stray from God.

Chill. Your brain is the product of the past. Reread that: your brain is anachronistic and outmoded.

We get to live in the future now. Count yourself lucky. You're one of the few that got to witness the birth of flight culminating in landing on the moon, except in this case your floppy disk became your personal shopper and stay-at-home Michelin star chef and part time driver.

It's okay to feel a bit of discomfort. It's hard to believe this is actually happening in our lifetimes, but it is! And that's so cool.

So sit back and enjoy the future.

sentences.en: This discourse reminds me of the handful of Christians who thought we needed Corn Flakes and uncomfortable clothing to stay chaste and that electricity made us stray from God.Chill.
sentences.zh: 这个演讲让我想起了少数基督徒，他们认为我们需要玉米片和不舒服的衣服来保持贞洁，而电会让我们远离上帝。冷静。

sentences.en: Your brain is the product of the past.
sentences.zh: 你的大脑是过去的产物。

sentences.en: Reread that: your brain is anachronistic and outmoded.We get to live in the future now.
sentences.zh: 重读一下：你的大脑已经不合时宜且过时了。我们现在就生活在未来。

sentences.en: Count yourself lucky.
sentences.zh: 算你自己幸运吧。

sentences.en: You're one of the few that got to witness the birth of flight culminating in landing on the moon, except in this case your floppy disk became your personal shopper and stay-at-home Michelin star chef and part time driver.It's okay to feel a bit of discomfort.
sentences.zh: 您是少数见证飞行诞生并最终登陆月球的人之一，但在这种情况下，您的软盘成为您的私人购物者、家庭米其林星级厨师和兼职司机。感到有点不舒服是可以接受的。

sentences.en: It's hard to believe this is actually happening in our lifetimes, but it is!
sentences.zh: 很难相信这真的发生在我们的有生之年，但它确实如此！

sentences.en: And that's so cool.So sit back and enjoy the future.
sentences.zh: 这太酷了。所以坐下来享受未来吧。

sentences.en: You're one of the few that got to witness the birth of flight culminating in landing on the moon, except in this case your floppy disk became your personal shopper and stay-at-home Michelin star chef and part time driver.
sentences.zh: 您是少数见证飞行诞生并最终登陆月球的人之一，但在这种情况下，您的软盘成为您的私人购物者、米其林星级厨师和兼职司机。

blocks.html_en: It's okay to feel a bit of discomfort. It's hard to believe this is actually happening in our lifetimes, but it is! And that's so cool.

sentences.en: It's okay to feel a bit of discomfort.
sentences.zh: 感到有点不舒服是可以的。

sentences.en: It's hard to believe this is actually happening in our lifetimes, but it is!
sentences.zh: 很难相信这真的发生在我们的有生之年，但它确实如此！

sentences.en: And that's so cool.
sentences.zh: 那太酷了。

blocks.html_en: So sit back and enjoy the future.

sentences.en: So sit back and enjoy the future.
sentences.zh: 所以坐下来享受未来吧。

blocks.html_en:
For a bit of context there are some classically very hard things going on in this video, so if you're not impressed, be glad you didn't have to try to build this 10 years ago.

- Coordinating two hands

- Mobile body

- Deformable objects

- Thin affordances (bag zip in particular)

- Multi-object single grasp

Really a host of things that had individually been tackled as either perception or manipulation problems and probably led to more than one person's PhD here on HN.

sentences.en: For a bit of context there are some classically very hard things going on in this video, so if you're not impressed, be glad you didn't have to try to build this 10 years ago.- Coordinating two hands- Mobile body- Deformable objects- Thin affordances (bag zip in particular)- Multi-object single graspReally a host of things that had individually been tackled as either perception or manipulation problems and probably led to more than one person's PhD here on HN.
sentences.zh: 对于一些背景知识，这个视频中发生了一些经典的非常困难的事情，所以如果你没有印象深刻，庆幸你不必在 10 年前尝试构建这个。 - 协调两只手 - 移动身体 - 可变形物体 - 薄可供性（特别是袋子拉链） - 多物体单一抓握实际上是一系列单独作为感知或操纵问题来解决的事情，并且可能导致不止一个人在 HN 上获得博士学位。

blocks.html_en: Watching the two-handed robot has me thinking that a third hand, perhaps with a different set of capabilities (like a tentacle grip) might make many tasks easier. I realise that more limbs = more problems, but even so, having additional ways to hold and turn things would be a big help.

sentences.en: Watching the two-handed robot has me thinking that a third hand, perhaps with a different set of capabilities (like a tentacle grip) might make many tasks easier.
sentences.zh: 看着双手机器人，我想到第三只手，也许具有不同的功能（如触手抓握），可能会使许多任务变得更容易。

sentences.en: I realise that more limbs = more problems, but even so, having additional ways to hold and turn things would be a big help.
sentences.zh: 我意识到更多的肢体=更多的问题，但即便如此，拥有额外的方法来握住和转动物体也会有很大的帮助。

blocks.html_en:
SciFi novel "The Mote in God's Eye." has aliens with three hands.

One "gripping hand" kinda coming out the gut, and two dextrous hands for detail work. Led to abuse of English language with: On the one hand... On the other hand... On the gripping hand...

sentences.en: SciFi novel "The Mote in God's Eye."
sentences.zh: 科幻小说《上帝眼中的微粒》。

sentences.en: has aliens with three hands.One "gripping hand" kinda coming out the gut, and two dextrous hands for detail work.
sentences.zh: 外星人有三只手。一只“抓握的手”有点从肠道里出来，两只灵巧的手负责细节工作。

sentences.en: Led to abuse of English language with: On the one hand...
sentences.zh: 导致英语语言的滥用：一方面......

sentences.en: On the other hand...
sentences.zh: 另一方面...

sentences.en: On the gripping hand...
sentences.zh: 紧握的手上……

blocks.html_en:
Which is reall just a patch for the problem of "On one hand… On the other hand… …On the OTHER hand…".

It's worth noting that the one-other-gripping pattern seems meant to convey two considerations and end with the strongest of three.

The English one-other pattern doesn't necessarily require the second consideration to dominate. They could be equal, or they might merely introduce concerns that haven't been evaluated to conclusion yet.

Alas, one-other-gripping doesn't seem to generalise to more than three concerns.

sentences.en: Which is reall just a patch for the problem of "On one hand…
sentences.zh: 这实际上只是解决“一方面......

sentences.en: On the other hand… …On the OTHER hand…".It's worth noting that the one-other-gripping pattern seems meant to convey two considerations and end with the strongest of three.The English one-other pattern doesn't necessarily require the second consideration to dominate.
sentences.zh: 另一方面…………另一方面……”。值得注意的是，one-other-gripping 模式似乎意味着传达两个考虑因素，并以三个因素中最强的一个结束。英语的 one-other 模式并不一定需要第二个考虑因素来占主导地位。

sentences.en: They could be equal, or they might merely introduce concerns that haven't been evaluated to conclusion yet.Alas, one-other-gripping doesn't seem to generalise to more than three concerns.
sentences.zh: 它们可能是相等的，或者它们可能只是引入尚未评估得出结论的担忧。唉，“彼此紧握”似乎不能概括为超过三个担忧。

blocks.html_en:
Coining the term: Slopfold

When you Robot folds your clothes but it's kinda wrinkly and sloppy but you accept it anyway because it's easier than precisely folded and pressed clothes.

sentences.en: Coining the term: SlopfoldWhen you Robot folds your clothes but it's kinda wrinkly and sloppy but you accept it anyway because it's easier than precisely folded and pressed clothes.
sentences.zh: 创造这个术语：Slopfold 当你的机器人折叠你的衣服时，它有点褶皱和草率，但你无论如何都会接受它，因为它比精确折叠和熨烫的衣服更容易。

blocks.html_en: Anecdotal, but the best machine will never be able to do something as good as the best human in most circumstances. That is because, unlike machines, humans have passions and character that subtly imbues itself in the end result. This, of course, isn't limited to coffee, but to anything a machine could do that isn't strictly tied to exercises in logic (for example, computers will always be better than humans at most games because they can be analysed logically and acted upon)

sentences.en: Anecdotal, but the best machine will never be able to do something as good as the best human in most circumstances.
sentences.zh: 这是轶事，但在大多数情况下，最好的机器永远无法做得像最好的人类一样好。

sentences.en: That is because, unlike machines, humans have passions and character that subtly imbues itself in the end result.
sentences.zh: 这是因为，与机器不同，人类拥有激情和性格，这些激情和性格巧妙地融入了最终结果。

> for example, computers will always be better than humans at most games because they can be analysed logically and acted upon

This made me wonder how we can imbue some of the the reasoning in your post to it. A game that values human ingenuity

blocks.html_en: I feel like, despite not being my (and presumably most of HNs, given the general demographics) cup of tea, sports are largely that. The players are skilled but also uniquely themselves. The world cup wouldn't be nearly as interesting if it were just unthinking robots fighting to see who can run around and kick things the best

sentences.en: I feel like, despite not being my (and presumably most of HNs, given the general demographics) cup of tea, sports are largely that.
sentences.zh: 我觉得，尽管不是我（考虑到一般人口统计数据，大概是大多数东道主）喜欢的运动，但体育运动在很大程度上就是这样的。

sentences.en: The players are skilled but also uniquely themselves.
sentences.zh: 球员们技术精湛，但也很独特。

sentences.en: The world cup wouldn't be nearly as interesting if it were just unthinking robots fighting to see who can run around and kick things the best
sentences.zh: 如果世界杯只是不假思索的机器人争夺谁能跑动和踢得最好，那么世界杯就不会那么有趣了

blocks.html_en:
Speaking as a relatively unprivileged kid from Southeast Asia, my parents were so busy working they just didn't have time and energy to care about me, let alone how well i fold my laundry.

But that's just one data point.

blocks.html_en: When AI is a the forefront of scientific breakthroughs and all human endeavor is painfully minuscule compared to AI, we'll still be calling it slop as we all hypocritically generate more and more slop.

blocks.html_en:
Nobody is proud of this. Where the hell did you get that idea?

I think weak and scared people tend to not want to face the truth about AI. I said the above not because I support it (I don't), I said it because it's the god awful truth. All trendlines point to steadily improving AI, and tons and tons of people continue to call it slop even though it is clearly becoming better.

My question for you is, in order to sleep at night do you have to lie to yourself? Do you make up stories about how AI will never be as good as human intelligence and do you assume anyone who says the contrary is an ardent supporter of AI? Have you thought that... maybe nobody really supports AI, they're just telling the truth?

Weird my ass, just stupid people who clearly assume the wrong thing.

blocks.html_en:
The uncut footage is so awesome.

Of course, you can mention all the things they can't do (what if there are stairs, what if the doors are smaller than the robot, etc) but it's mindblowing to see what they can do - and also the chat panel is really nice and makes it more interactive.

sentences.en: The uncut footage is so awesome.Of course, you can mention all the things they can't do (what if there are stairs, what if the doors are smaller than the robot, etc) but it's mindblowing to see what they can do - and also the chat panel is really nice and makes it more interactive.
sentences.zh: 未剪辑的镜头太棒了。当然，你可以提到他们不能做的所有事情（如果有楼梯怎么办，如果门比机器人小怎么办，等等），但看到他们能做的事情真是令人兴奋——而且聊天面板也非常好，使其更具互动性。

blocks.html_en:
The uncut footage is so awesome.

Of course, you can mention all the things they can't do (what if there are stairs, what if the doors are smaller than the robot, etc) but it's mindblowing to see what they can do - and also the chat panel is really nice and makes it more interactive.

sentences.en: Of course, you can mention all the things they can't do (what if there are stairs, what if the doors are smaller than the robot, etc) but it's mindblowing to see what they can do - and also the chat panel is really nice and makes it more interactive.
sentences.zh: 当然，你可以提到他们不能做的所有事情（如果有楼梯怎么办，如果门比机器人小怎么办等等），但看到他们能做的事情是令人兴奋的 - 而且聊天面板非常好，使其更具互动性。

blocks.html_en: Designing a non humanoid robot is the way to go. The design of new tools and appliances for hybrid human/robot use will eventually follow: the zip line for the shoe bag required way too much precision work.

sentences.en: Designing a non humanoid robot is the way to go.
sentences.zh: 设计一个非人形机器人是一条出路。

sentences.en: The design of new tools and appliances for hybrid human/robot use will eventually follow: the zip line for the shoe bag required way too much precision work.
sentences.zh: 人类/机器人混合使用的新工具和设备的设计最终将随之而来：鞋袋的滑索需要太多的精确工作。

blocks.html_en:
I think this perspective is now demonstrably false. It was basically the defacto position of most roboticists until a few months back. But we've already seen the cost of humanoid robots fall precipitously (even without the kind of mass production that genuinely useful home and real world intelligence would bring).

Which seems more likely, that cheap humanoids will continue to improve in dexterity, battery life, strength etc. Leveraging the vast investment in research into real world task training. Or - that we'll redesign millions of everyday objects to fit the limitations of current task specific robots?

If you answer the latter, I've got a whole bunch of mid century quirky labour saving inventions in the back room I'd like to sell you.

sentences.en: I think this perspective is now demonstrably false.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种观点现在显然是错误的。

sentences.en: It was basically the defacto position of most roboticists until a few months back.
sentences.zh: 直到几个月前，这基本上是大多数机器人专家事实上的立场。

sentences.en: But we've already seen the cost of humanoid robots fall precipitously (even without the kind of mass production that genuinely useful home and real world intelligence would bring).Which seems more likely, that cheap humanoids will continue to improve in dexterity, battery life, strength etc. Leveraging the vast investment in research into real world task training.
sentences.zh: 但我们已经看到人形机器人的成本急剧下降（即使没有真正有用的家庭和现实世界智能所带来的大规模生产）。更有可能的是，廉价的人形机器人将在灵活性、电池寿命、力量等方面继续提高。利用对现实世界任务训练研究的巨额投资。

sentences.en: Or - that we'll redesign millions of everyday objects to fit the limitations of current task specific robots?If you answer the latter, I've got a whole bunch of mid century quirky labour saving inventions in the back room I'd like to sell you.
sentences.zh: 或者 - 我们将重新设计数以百万计的日常用品，以适应当前任务特定机器人的局限性？如果你回答后者，我想把一大堆中世纪古怪的节省劳动力的发明放在后面的房间里，我想卖给你。

blocks.html_en:
It’s not demonstrated yet as we still have to see a robot (humanoid or not) doing mundane tasks in full autonomy, efficiently and convincingly.

It still looks inefficient to have a humanoid robot vacuum a house by using an ergonomic tool designed around the human body.

sentences.en: It’s not demonstrated yet as we still have to see a robot (humanoid or not) doing mundane tasks in full autonomy, efficiently and convincingly.It still looks inefficient to have a humanoid robot vacuum a house by using an ergonomic tool designed around the human body.
sentences.zh: 它还没有得到证实，因为我们仍然需要看到一个机器人（无论是否是人形机器人）完全自主、高效、令人信服地完成日常任务。让人形机器人使用围绕人体设计的人体工学工具来打扫房子，看起来仍然效率低下。

blocks.html_en:
I can't believe someone shares my point of view. We have already many non humanoid robots to do boring stuff (underwater maintenance, stuff like that). For some reasons people tend to think that for robots to do stuff close to humans, we need human like robots.

We do not. I want a swarm of insects-like robots to fight mosquitos. A snake one that fixes my pipe. A spider-like robot that cleans surfaces. Etc.

sentences.en: I can't believe someone shares my point of view.
sentences.zh: 我不敢相信有人同意我的观点。

sentences.en: We have already many non humanoid robots to do boring stuff (underwater maintenance, stuff like that).
sentences.zh: 我们已经有很多非人形机器人来做无聊的事情（水下维护，诸如此类的事情）。

sentences.en: For some reasons people tend to think that for robots to do stuff close to humans, we need human like robots.We do not.
sentences.zh: 由于某些原因，人们倾向于认为，为了让机器人做与人类接近的事情，我们需要像机器人一样的人类。但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: I want a swarm of insects-like robots to fight mosquitos.
sentences.zh: 我想要一群像昆虫一样的机器人来对抗蚊子。

sentences.en: A snake one that fixes my pipe.
sentences.zh: 一条修我的烟斗的蛇。

sentences.en: A spider-like robot that cleans surfaces.
sentences.zh: 一个像蜘蛛一样的机器人，可以清洁表面。

sentences.en: Etc.
sentences.zh: ETC。

blocks.html_en: I will suggest mix of both: some everyday tools are already built for being used with hands and legs (e.g opening a door). But indeed adding some different grip types, additional arms, etc, could be helpful.

sentences.zh: 我建议将两者结合起来：一些日常工具已经被设计成可以用手和腿使用（例如开门）。

sentences.en: But indeed adding some different grip types, additional arms, etc, could be helpful.
sentences.zh: 但事实上，添加一些不同的抓握类型、额外的手臂等可能会有所帮助。

blocks.html_en: Exactly what I came here to say. A robot doesn't have to look like a human. Just a few arms that does the job neatly is more than enough.

sentences.zh: 这正是我来这里要说的。

sentences.zh: 机器人不必看起来像人类。

sentences.en: Just a few arms that does the job neatly is more than enough.
sentences.zh: 只需几只手臂就能巧妙地完成这项工作就足够了。

blocks.html_en: This is amazing! I can see in a few years having Home Assistant use an LLM to coordinate my home while I'm away. Tell the LLM to make an inventory of my home and then send the vacuum and the robot around to clean everything up. A multimodal LLM can inspect pictures for items that need to go in the dishwasher, the laundry or needs polishing and reorganise my closets to best allocate space, make the beds, take care of the plants. It has all day to do it while the solar panels provide ample energy anyway.

sentences.en: This is amazing!
sentences.zh: 这太棒了！

sentences.en: I can see in a few years having Home Assistant use an LLM to coordinate my home while I'm away.
sentences.zh: 我预计几年后，家庭助理会在我外出时使用法学硕士来协调我的家庭。

sentences.en: Tell the LLM to make an inventory of my home and then send the vacuum and the robot around to clean everything up.
sentences.zh: 告诉法学硕士对我的家进行清点，然后派出吸尘器和机器人来清理所有东西。

sentences.en: A multimodal LLM can inspect pictures for items that need to go in the dishwasher, the laundry or needs polishing and reorganise my closets to best allocate space, make the beds, take care of the plants.
sentences.zh: 多模式法学硕士可以检查图片中是否有需要放入洗碗机、洗衣房或需要抛光的物品，并重新整理我的壁橱以最好地分配空间、铺床、照顾植物。

sentences.en: It has all day to do it while the solar panels provide ample energy anyway.
sentences.zh: 它有一整天的时间来做这件事，而太阳能电池板无论如何都能提供充足的能量。

blocks.html_en: Does this require a given hardware platform, or for example, could I run this model with a cheap mini robot with a single camera (i.e. good enough without depth), tracked treads, and an arm?

blocks.html_en: I have Roborock (Xiaomi) automatic vacuum and it's fantastic technology. iRobot had a 10 year lead and did nothing with it. The robot is competent at avoiding clutter, clean the mops itself, leave my floor sparkling and so far has been extremely reliable.

blocks.html_en:
Got two Xiaomi phones in a row, one in 2018 for 250 euros (note 8 pro) and one in 2023 for 300 (13T) and I liked them a lot.

My gripe with the second one is lowish battery life, it always bites me on longer trips when my gf's Pixel 8 lasts considerably more, but was also way more expensive.

Will check battery reviews better next time I shop for a phone, I don't care much for the rest.

sentences.en: Got two Xiaomi phones in a row, one in 2018 for 250 euros (note 8 pro) and one in 2023 for 300 (13T) and I liked them a lot.My gripe with the second one is lowish battery life, it always bites me on longer trips when my gf's Pixel 8 lasts considerably more, but was also way more expensive.Will check battery reviews better next time I shop for a phone, I don't care much for the rest.
sentences.zh: 连续买了两部小米手机，一部是 2018 年的 250 欧元（note 8 pro），一部是 2023 年的 300 欧元（13T），我非常喜欢它们。我对第二部的抱怨是电池续航时间太短，当我女朋友的 Pixel 8 续航时间长得多时，它总是让我在长途旅行中受到困扰，但它也贵得多。下次我买手机时会更好地检查电池评论，我不太关心其余的。

blocks.html_en: How are these models compared to world models by Waymo and Tesla, assuming their models are trained for recognising objects that can be further generalized for everyday chores?

sentences.zh: 假设这些模型经过训练可以识别可以进一步推广到日常琐事的物体，那么这些模型与 Waymo 和 Tesla 的世界模型相比如何？

blocks.html_en:
Compared how? Architecturally? In training methods? In performance?

The remarkable thing about this modern "full VLA" approach is that it doesn't train for an explicit "world model" at all. There's no factored world state, no pool of trajectory rollouts being sampled from. Instead, everything is rolled into the same VLA stack that does all of it internally.

The world model exists, but it's implicit - the VLA learns one, but only because it helps its training objectives. Trajectories are planned ahead sometimes, also implicitly, for the same reasons.

It's a very "bitter lesson" way of doing things.

As far as I know, Tesla's stack is somewhat similar architecturally - a highly integrated end to end model - but the training process for FSD does include factored "world modeling" side objectives to force the stack to develop driving-relevant internal world representations. So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.

Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.

sentences.en: Compared how?
sentences.zh: 比较如何？

sentences.en: Architecturally?
sentences.zh: 建筑学上？

sentences.en: In training methods?
sentences.zh: 在训练方法上？

sentences.en: In performance?The remarkable thing about this modern "full VLA" approach is that it doesn't train for an explicit "world model" at all.
sentences.zh: 在性能方面？这种现代“完整 VLA”方法的显着之处在于，它根本不针对明确的“世界模型”进行训练。

sentences.en: There's no factored world state, no pool of trajectory rollouts being sampled from.
sentences.zh: 没有因子化的世界状态，也没有从中采样的轨迹推出池。

sentences.en: Instead, everything is rolled into the same VLA stack that does all of it internally.The world model exists, but it's implicit - the VLA learns one, but only because it helps its training objectives.
sentences.zh: 相反，所有内容都被卷入同一个 VLA 堆栈中，该堆栈在内部完成所有操作。世界模型存在，但它是隐含的 - VLA 学习一个模型，但只是因为它有助于实现其训练目标。

sentences.en: Trajectories are planned ahead sometimes, also implicitly, for the same reasons.It's a very "bitter lesson" way of doing things.As far as I know, Tesla's stack is somewhat similar architecturally - a highly integrated end to end model - but the training process for FSD does include factored "world modeling" side objectives to force the stack to develop driving-relevant internal world representations.
sentences.zh: 出于同样的原因，有时也会隐式地提前计划轨迹。这是一种非常“惨痛的教训”的做事方式。据我所知，特斯拉的堆栈在架构上有些相似 - 一个高度集成的端到端模型 - 但 FSD 的训练过程确实包括分解的“世界建模”侧面目标，以迫使堆栈开发与驾驶相关的内部世界表示。

sentences.en: So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.
sentences.zh: 因此，它处于经典的“世界模型”系统和 VLA 风格之间的中间立场 - 尽管令人惊讶地接近后者。世界建模方面的目标是否有原则性和合理性，还是规模不足时代的遗留问题，尚不清楚。

blocks.html_en:
Compared how? Architecturally? In training methods? In performance?

The remarkable thing about this modern "full VLA" approach is that it doesn't train for an explicit "world model" at all. There's no factored world state, no pool of trajectory rollouts being sampled from. Instead, everything is rolled into the same VLA stack that does all of it internally.

The world model exists, but it's implicit - the VLA learns one, but only because it helps its training objectives. Trajectories are planned ahead sometimes, also implicitly, for the same reasons.

It's a very "bitter lesson" way of doing things.

As far as I know, Tesla's stack is somewhat similar architecturally - a highly integrated end to end model - but the training process for FSD does include factored "world modeling" side objectives to force the stack to develop driving-relevant internal world representations. So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.

Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.

sentences.zh: 出于同样的原因，有时也会隐式地提前计划轨迹。这是一种非常“惨痛的教训”的做事方式。据我所知，特斯拉的堆栈在架构上有些相似 - 一个高度集成的端到端模型 - 但 FSD 的训练过程确实包括分解的“世界建模”侧面目标，以迫使堆栈开发与驾驶相关的内部世界表示。

sentences.en: So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.
sentences.zh: 因此，它处于经典的“世界模型”系统和 VLA 风格之间的中间立场 - 尽管令人惊讶地接近后者。世界建模方面的目标是否有原则性和合理性，还是规模不足时代的遗留问题，尚不清楚。

blocks.html_en: Compared how? Architecturally? In training methods? In performance?

sentences.en: Compared how?
sentences.zh: 比较如何？

sentences.en: Architecturally?
sentences.zh: 建筑学上？

sentences.en: In training methods?
sentences.zh: 在训练方法上？

sentences.en: In performance?
sentences.zh: 表现上？

blocks.html_en: The remarkable thing about this modern "full VLA" approach is that it doesn't train for an explicit "world model" at all. There's no factored world state, no pool of trajectory rollouts being sampled from. Instead, everything is rolled into the same VLA stack that does all of it internally.

sentences.en: The remarkable thing about this modern "full VLA" approach is that it doesn't train for an explicit "world model" at all.
sentences.zh: 这种现代“完整 VLA”方法的显着之处在于，它根本不针对明确的“世界模型”进行训练。

sentences.en: There's no factored world state, no pool of trajectory rollouts being sampled from.
sentences.zh: 没有因子化的世界状态，也没有从中采样的轨迹推出池。

sentences.en: Instead, everything is rolled into the same VLA stack that does all of it internally.
sentences.zh: 相反，所有内容都被汇总到同一个 VLA 堆栈中，该堆栈在内部完成所有操作。

blocks.html_en: The world model exists, but it's implicit - the VLA learns one, but only because it helps its training objectives. Trajectories are planned ahead sometimes, also implicitly, for the same reasons.

sentences.en: The world model exists, but it's implicit - the VLA learns one, but only because it helps its training objectives.
sentences.zh: 世界模型存在，但它是隐含的 - VLA 学习一个世界模型，但只是因为它有助于其训练目标。

sentences.en: Trajectories are planned ahead sometimes, also implicitly, for the same reasons.
sentences.zh: 出于同样的原因，有时也会隐式地提前计划轨迹。

blocks.html_en: It's a very "bitter lesson" way of doing things.

sentences.en: It's a very "bitter lesson" way of doing things.
sentences.zh: 这是一个非常“惨痛的教训”的做事方式。

blocks.html_en: As far as I know, Tesla's stack is somewhat similar architecturally - a highly integrated end to end model - but the training process for FSD does include factored "world modeling" side objectives to force the stack to develop driving-relevant internal world representations. So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.

sentences.en: As far as I know, Tesla's stack is somewhat similar architecturally - a highly integrated end to end model - but the training process for FSD does include factored "world modeling" side objectives to force the stack to develop driving-relevant internal world representations.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，特斯拉的堆栈在架构上有些相似 - 一个高度集成的端到端模型 - 但 FSD 的训练过程确实包括分解的“世界建模”侧面目标，以迫使堆栈开发与驾驶相关的内部世界表示。

sentences.en: So it's in a middle ground between a classic "world model" system and this flavor of VLA - albeit surprisingly close to the latter.
sentences.zh: 因此，它处于经典的“世界模型”系统和 VLA 风格之间的中间地带 - 尽管令人惊讶地接近后者。

blocks.html_en: Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.

sentences.en: Whether the world modeling side objectives are principled and justified or a leftover from the days of insufficient scale is unclear.
sentences.zh: 世界建模方面的目标是否有原则、合理，还是规模不足时期的遗留问题，目前尚不清楚。

blocks.html_en:
This is very cool, and the paradigm that scales to bringing truly useful robots to virtually all domains of life - just as LLMs have scaled to that degree across virtually all domains of knowledge work.

I picture a lot of perfectly tended gardens growing food.

blocks.html_en:
How dare Xiaomi post a realistic picture of where modern consumer robotics is at.

They will never become trillionaires if they don't pretend they are making a million humanoid robots by end of the year.

blocks.html_en:
Pretty cool!

Only 10B parameters if I correctly understand some of those histograms, which is tiny in LLM world! It bodes well for the future.

The training videos seem to show human hands using the robot grippers to execute the tasks, which must have been a fun but tedious process.

sentences.en: Pretty cool!Only 10B parameters if I correctly understand some of those histograms, which is tiny in LLM world!
sentences.zh: 太酷了！如果我正确理解其中一些直方图的话，只有 10B 个参数，这在 LLM 世界中是很小的！

sentences.en: It bodes well for the future.The training videos seem to show human hands using the robot grippers to execute the tasks, which must have been a fun but tedious process.
sentences.zh: 这对未来来说是个好兆头。培训视频似乎展示了人手使用机器人抓手来执行任务，这一定是一个有趣但乏味的过程。

blocks.html_en:
Yes, it's truly remarkable how small modern VLAs are.

Even Tesla's FSD AI stack is small by modern LLM standards. Once the hardware is there to enable real time 100B inference on endpoint SBCs? We'd see gains in AI robotics from that alone.

sentences.en: Yes, it's truly remarkable how small modern VLAs are.Even Tesla's FSD AI stack is small by modern LLM standards.
sentences.zh: 是的，现代 VLA 是如此之小，确实令人惊叹。按照现代 LLM 标准，即使是特斯拉的 FSD AI 堆栈也很小。

sentences.en: Once the hardware is there to enable real time 100B inference on endpoint SBCs?
sentences.zh: 一旦硬件能够在端点 SBC 上启用实时 100B 推理？

sentences.en: We'd see gains in AI robotics from that alone.
sentences.zh: 仅此一点我们就能看到人工智能机器人技术的进步。

At what point are we gonna start seeing American firms attending WAIC?

blocks.html_en:
Considering that they pre-trained on non-robot data, and mid-trained on data from other robot types? I suspect that generalization would be decent for most similar platforms - things with 1 to 2 arms and 2-finger hands.

You'd probably have to fine tune for good performance on a specific arm type. But this model isn't trained against hard assumptions of what the underlying hardware is.

sentences.en: Considering that they pre-trained on non-robot data, and mid-trained on data from other robot types?
sentences.zh: 考虑到他们对非机器人数据进行预训练，并对其他机器人类型的数据进行中期训练？

sentences.en: But this model isn't trained against hard assumptions of what the underlying hardware is.
sentences.zh: 但该模型并未针对底层硬件的严格假设进行训练。

It was still satisfying.

blocks.html_en:
Because you see a robot do one thing, doesn't mean it can do other things. We've had good robot demos for decades.

For all we know they could've spent 5 years and 10 million dollars on getting this robot to fold laundry. That doesn't mean it'll be able to mop the floor better than a Roomba in a decade.

sentences.en: Because you see a robot do one thing, doesn't mean it can do other things.
sentences.zh: 因为你看到机器人做一件事，并不意味着它可以做其他事情。

sentences.en: We've had good robot demos for decades.For all we know they could've spent 5 years and 10 million dollars on getting this robot to fold laundry.
sentences.zh: 几十年来，我们已经进行了很好的机器人演示。据我们所知，他们可能会花费 5 年时间和 1000 万美元来让这个机器人折叠衣服。

sentences.en: That doesn't mean it'll be able to mop the floor better than a Roomba in a decade.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着十年后它就能比 Roomba 更好地拖地。

blocks.html_en: No we haven't. Back in the 90's Honda's Asimov could barely walk. Now we have robots managing to run marathons. Clothes folding had to be played back at 50x speed because the compute just wasn't there yet. It still is barely there, some of those videos are sped up. They've probably spent way more than 5 years and 10 million dollars developing that robot. Roomba already came out with a mopping robot, why would you want this one to do that?

blocks.html_en:
They're trying to show their investors a butler robot that can do all types of household chores.

Folding fabric has traditionally been very hard to solve with robots; the ideal places to grab the fabric might be hidden, the fabric patterns are repetitive, and the material flops around, tangles in the grippers, and brings other pieces of laundry with it.

Successfully sorting and folding a basket of laundry shows they've got a control system capable of performing some quite difficult tasks.

sentences.en: They're trying to show their investors a butler robot that can do all types of household chores.Folding fabric has traditionally been very hard to solve with robots; the ideal places to grab the fabric might be hidden, the fabric patterns are repetitive, and the material flops around, tangles in the grippers, and brings other pieces of laundry with it.Successfully sorting and folding a basket of laundry shows they've got a control system capable of performing some quite difficult tasks.
sentences.zh: 他们试图向投资者展示一款可以做所有类型家务的管家机器人。传统上，折叠织物很难用机器人来解决；但现在，它已经成为了人们关注的焦点。抓取织物的理想位置可能是隐藏的，织物图案是重复的，并且材料会四处翻滚，缠结在夹具中，并带走其他衣物。成功分类和折叠一篮子衣物表明他们拥有能够执行一些相当困难的任务的控制系统。

blocks.html_en:
They're trying to show their investors a butler robot that can do all types of household chores.

Folding fabric has traditionally been very hard to solve with robots; the ideal places to grab the fabric might be hidden, the fabric patterns are repetitive, and the material flops around, tangles in the grippers, and brings other pieces of laundry with it.

Successfully sorting and folding a basket of laundry shows they've got a control system capable of performing some quite difficult tasks.

sentences.en: They're trying to show their investors a butler robot that can do all types of household chores.Folding fabric has traditionally been very hard to solve with robots; the ideal places to grab the fabric might be hidden, the fabric patterns are repetitive, and the material flops around, tangles in the grippers, and brings other pieces of laundry with it.Successfully sorting and folding a basket of laundry shows they've got a control system capable of performing some quite difficult tasks.
sentences.zh: 他们试图向投资者展示一款可以做所有类型家务的管家机器人。传统上，折叠织物很难用机器人来解决；但现在，它已经成为了人们关注的焦点。抓取织物的理想位置可能是隐藏的，织物图案是重复的，并且材料会四处翻滚，缠结在夹具中，并带走其他衣物。成功分类和折叠一篮子衣物表明他们拥有能够执行一些相当困难的任务的控制系统。

blocks.html_en:
All you need to do to understand what task is more complex (for a robot to solve) is just to ask a 6-8 years old child to do it. It takes days before they "understand".

So it cannot be that simple (for the reasons you mentioned).

sentences.zh: 他们需要几天的时间才能“理解”。所以事情不可能那么简单（出于您提到的原因）。

blocks.html_en: As a parent with 3 kids and both my wife and I working, folding clothes is the most tedious household chore. It's physically and mentally exhausting (unlike doing the dishes, which is only physically exhausting as I can wash piles of dishes while zoning out). And not to mention take so much time, a week's worth of unfolded pile of clothes for our entire household can take me 1-2 hours to fold uninterrupted (2-3 hours if I do it while watching Netflix or something).

sentences.en: As a parent with 3 kids and both my wife and I working, folding clothes is the most tedious household chore.
sentences.zh: 作为一个有 3 个孩子的父母，我和妻子都工作，叠衣服是最繁琐的家务活。

sentences.en: It's physically and mentally exhausting (unlike doing the dishes, which is only physically exhausting as I can wash piles of dishes while zoning out).
sentences.zh: 这让身体和精神都疲惫不堪（不像洗碗，洗碗只是体力上的疲惫，因为我可以一边走神一边洗成堆的盘子）。

sentences.en: And not to mention take so much time, a week's worth of unfolded pile of clothes for our entire household can take me 1-2 hours to fold uninterrupted (2-3 hours if I do it while watching Netflix or something).
sentences.zh: 更不用说要花这么多时间了，就我们全家一周展开的一堆衣服而言，我可能需要 1-2 个小时不间断地折叠（如果我一边看 Netflix 或其他东西一边折叠，则需要 2-3 小时）。

blocks.html_en: Small sharing tips, in case it can help: have the maximum of hangers possible. It take a bit more space, but the time saved is huge + it remove iron time for low wrinkle sensitive clothes (e.g cotton polo)

sentences.en: Small sharing tips, in case it can help: have the maximum of hangers possible.
sentences.zh: 分享一些小技巧（如果有帮助的话）：尽可能多地放置衣架。

sentences.en: It take a bit more space, but the time saved is huge + it remove iron time for low wrinkle sensitive clothes (e.g cotton polo)
sentences.zh: 它需要更多的空间，但节省的时间是巨大的+它消除了低皱纹敏感衣服（例如棉质 Polo 衫）的熨烫时间

blocks.html_en: Yep.. and with 3 kids so many of the time-consuming "tasks" are not open to automation because a human doing it is the point.. eg. bedtime books, bathing the kids, helping them make their lunch/breakfast, etc.

sentences.zh: 是的..

sentences.en: eg.

sentences.zh: 睡前书籍、给孩子们洗澡、帮助他们做午餐/早餐等。

Doing household chores is good for the kids. I wouldn't wan't this robot driving around my household when I have small children anyway...

blocks.html_en: I mean folding after it has been dried. I hate folding clothes. In my case luckily my wife doesn't mind (as I don't mind doing the rest of the process) but if in say 25 years from now there will be a relatively small and affordable folding appliance, I would totally buy it.

blocks.html_en:
80% will be in the laundry bin, your first task before doing anything is either shouting around the house to bring in the other 80% right now or go on expedition yourself.

10 minutes later you are at the washing machine, where you'll never be able to fit all laundry so it's two runs minimum. Somebody will hopefully in advance, but probably only prompted by your call around the house mention a number of particular items will be needed later today, certainly before the second load will be dry. You are now searching for those items. Or arguing for why you really don't have time and parents are humans too and not unpaid servants.

Another 10 minutes later the first load is washing. You set a timer, or perhaps have a machine that pings you when done. Of course, you have More Shit To Do, so you don't notice your alarm or ping and by the end of the morning when you hoped to sit down to enjoy maybe a whole 15 minutes of reading move the first load to the dryer and put the second load on. You discover that somebody never emptied the dryer. Finders keepers!

1,5hr later you fortunately do not miss the alarm and take our the dry but not 100% dry laundry and move the second load to dry.

3hr later you empty the second dryer load, because the run took longer because nobody ever fucking cleans the lint-container. At least you had time to fold (which to the many item-types in your household and your poor fine motor control certainly does not take you _just_ 15 minutes). A cat decides to kick over a pile and leave hair in a second pile, causing you to redo some work. 30 minutes in your are done and finally have time to fold the second driers worth.

Do you get it now?

sentences.en: 80% will be in the laundry bin, your first task before doing anything is either shouting around the house to bring in the other 80% right now or go on expedition yourself.10 minutes later you are at the washing machine, where you'll never be able to fit all laundry so it's two runs minimum.
sentences.zh: 80% 的衣物将放入洗衣桶中，在做任何事情之前，您的首要任务就是要么在屋子里大喊大叫，立即将另外 80% 的衣物带进来，要么自己去探险。 10 分钟后，您将到达洗衣机前，在那里您永远无法放入所有衣物，因此至少需要运行两次。

sentences.en: Somebody will hopefully in advance, but probably only prompted by your call around the house mention a number of particular items will be needed later today, certainly before the second load will be dry.
sentences.zh: 希望有人会提前，但可能只是因为你在家里打电话时提到今天晚些时候需要一些特殊物品，当然是在第二批货物变干之前。

sentences.en: You are now searching for those items.
sentences.zh: 您现在正在寻找这些物品。

sentences.en: Or arguing for why you really don't have time and parents are humans too and not unpaid servants.Another 10 minutes later the first load is washing.
sentences.zh: 或者争论为什么你真的没有时间，父母也是人，而不是无薪仆人。又过了 10 分钟，第一批衣服正在洗。

sentences.en: You set a timer, or perhaps have a machine that pings you when done.
sentences.zh: 你设置一个计时器，或者可能有一台机器在完成后通知你。

sentences.en: Of course, you have More Shit To Do, so you don't notice your alarm or ping and by the end of the morning when you hoped to sit down to enjoy maybe a whole 15 minutes of reading move the first load to the dryer and put the second load on.
sentences.zh: 当然，你还有更多的事情要做，所以你不会注意到你的闹钟或叮当声，到了早上，当你希望坐下来享受大约 15 分钟的阅读时，将第一个负载移到烘干机中，然后放入第二个负载。

sentences.en: You discover that somebody never emptied the dryer.
sentences.zh: 您发现有人从未清空烘干机。

sentences.en: Finders keepers!1,5hr later you fortunately do not miss the alarm and take our the dry but not 100% dry laundry and move the second load to dry.3hr later you empty the second dryer load, because the run took longer because nobody ever fucking cleans the lint-container.
sentences.zh: 发现者守护者！幸运的是，1.5 小时后，您没有错过警报，并拿起我们的干燥但不是 100% 干燥的衣物，并将第二个衣物移至干燥处。3 小时后，您清空了第二个烘干机负载，因为运行时间更长，因为没有人他妈的清洁棉绒容器。

sentences.en: At least you had time to fold (which to the many item-types in your household and your poor fine motor control certainly does not take you _just_ 15 minutes).
sentences.zh: 至少你有时间折叠（对于你家里的许多物品类型和你糟糕的精细运动控制来说，这肯定不会花费你_仅仅_ 15 分钟）。

sentences.en: A cat decides to kick over a pile and leave hair in a second pile, causing you to redo some work.
sentences.zh: 一只猫决定踢翻一堆毛发，将另一堆毛发留在其中，导致您需要重做一些工作。

sentences.en: 30 minutes in your are done and finally have time to fold the second driers worth.Do you get it now?
sentences.zh: 30 分钟后你就完成了，终于有时间折叠第二个烘干机了。你现在明白了吗？

blocks.html_en: Everybody's different, I guess, but for me this is absolutely a chore I would automate away, and I don't want to "force" another human being to do it for me.

blocks.html_en: A lot of smaller chores look trivial when considered in isolation, but have disproportionate time and mental cost because of how they fit in the flow of life. Sometimes the effect is similar to interrupting an engineer for a couple of minutes.

sentences.en: A lot of smaller chores look trivial when considered in isolation, but have disproportionate time and mental cost because of how they fit in the flow of life.
sentences.zh: 许多较小的家务事单独考虑时看起来微不足道，但由于它们如何融入生活流程，因此会花费不成比例的时间和精神成本。

sentences.en: Sometimes the effect is similar to interrupting an engineer for a couple of minutes.
sentences.zh: 有时，效果类似于打扰工程师几分钟。

I'm not super sure how relevant this is to the overall topic and thread, tbh. There's plenty of cheap yet real rebuttals, whether it's all the things with ICE (and not just the top-of-mind stuff; but effective deprivation of due process for suspects; etc), or even war crimes.

I don't think it serves much purpose tbh -- it's not going to change anyone's mind.

sentences.en: I'm not super sure how relevant this is to the overall topic and thread, tbh.
sentences.zh: 老实说，我不太确定这与整个主题和线程有多相关。

sentences.en: There's plenty of cheap yet real rebuttals, whether it's all the things with ICE (and not just the top-of-mind stuff; but effective deprivation of due process for suspects; etc), or even war crimes.I don't think it serves much purpose tbh -- it's not going to change anyone's mind.
sentences.zh: 有很多廉价但真实的反驳，无论是 ICE 的所有事情（不仅仅是最重要的事情；但有效地剥夺了嫌疑人的正当程序等），甚至是战争罪。说实话，我认为它没有多大作用——它不会改变任何人的想法。
